steps.title: Identify which works the notes call most famous
steps.content: The notes explicitly identify E.M. Forster's most famous novels as A Room with a View (1908) and Howards End (1910). To provide a specific example of one of his most famous works, the answer must reference one of these two novels.

steps.title: Find the choice that names one of these famous works
steps.content:
Choice C identifies A Room with a View and calls it "one of E.M. Forster's most famous works," which matches the notes exactly. The other choices fail: Choice A gives only general biographical information about Forster without naming a specific famous work. Choices B and D reference "Albergo Empedocle," which the notes identify as his first published work — not one of his most famous.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
